run.title: Agent Draft: Recruiting Ops Friction
run.intent: Assess repeated friction in internal recruiting operations and shortlist wedge candidates.
run.summary: Draft synthesis from structured source notes. Requires human review before acceptance.
run.recommendation: Watch and validate buyer urgency with recruiter operations leads.

warnings: Sample package uses synthetic IDs and should not be treated as accepted research.
limitations: No direct willingness-to-pay interviews included in this draft package.

sources.title: Hiring team forum thread on handoff delays
sources.summary: Recruiters report repeated delays when interview feedback is spread across tools.

sources.title: Recruiter interview notes
sources.summary: Small teams lose hours weekly reconciling conflicting candidate-status updates.

evidence_excerpts.excerpt_text: Status updates are split across ATS comments and chat threads, so nobody trusts the latest state.
I spend Friday afternoons just aligning interview notes and next-step ownership.

claims.statement: Recruiting teams face repeated coordination friction due to fragmented candidate-state updates.

themes.name: State-tracking fragmentation
themes.summary: Teams lack one trusted workflow for candidate progression and ownership.

candidates.title: Recruiting Handoff Ledger
candidates.summary: A narrow workflow layer that reconciles candidate-state updates and ownership transitions.
candidates.target_user: Recruiting coordinators at small to mid-size companies
candidates.first_wedge: Single state timeline with explicit owner transitions
candidates.why_it_might_fail: Teams may resist another dashboard unless it directly saves weekly reconciliation time.

validation_gates.name: Confirm buyer urgency
validation_gates.description: Interview at least three recruiting operations leads who own process tooling decisions.
validation_gates.missing_evidence: No direct buyer interviews in this draft package yet.
